title: 1.1: Reports Kitty checks /etc/xdg/kitty/kitty.conf as system-wide configuration first, then ~/.config/kitty/kitty.conf as user configuration, demonstrating configuration source hierarchy.
1.2: Reports init_glfw() selects platform module glfw-x11.so, glfw-wayland.so, or glfw-cocoa.so to determine which windowing system (X11, Wayland, or macOS) starts up
1.3: Reports text renders at configured font_size value (e.g., 11.0 pt or 18.0 pt) visible on screen when window appears, demonstrating font settings affect what you see
1.4: Reports load_all_shaders() compiles GPU shader programs (cell_vertex.glsl, cell_fragment.glsl, graphics shaders) during window creation, enabling GPU-accelerated rendering system
1.5: Reports log message [0.413] Child launched (or similar timestamp) appears in debug output, showing shell process started and came online
1.6: Reports openpty() creates master/slave file descriptors for PTY communication channel, answering how Kitty gets terminal ready to communicate with shell.
1.7: Reports TERM=xterm-kitty set in shell environment, answering how shell knows which escape sequences to send.
1.8: Reports TERMINFO=/app/terminfo (or base64 encoded terminfo data) set in shell environment, providing terminal capability definitions
1.9: Reports setting up Xvfb (virtual framebuffer) for headless execution with display configuration (e.g., DISPLAY=:99) before launching Kitty
1.10: Reports screen.mark_as_dirty() triggers when shell data arrives, causing display to redraw to show new shell output on screen
1.11: Reports escape sequence \e[31m displayed as red text on screen, demonstrating ANSI color codes are rendered.
1.12: Reports text scrolls upward without screen tearing or flickering when pressing Enter multiple times, demonstrating refresh cycle works.
1.13: Reports window appears on screen with configured background colour (such as #181818), confirming visual startup.
1.14: Reports cell grid layout visible with characters spaced at consistent cell width and height
1.15: Reports SIGWINCH signal sent to child process to communicate terminal size changes
2.1: States configuration files override command-line -o options
2.2: States fonts load after window appears on screen.
